title: Implementing a Basic Blog System using TypeORM
statement:
Create a RESTful API to manage a simple blog system using TypeORM. The system should allow creating, reading, updating, and deleting (CRUD) blog posts. Each post should have a title, content, and author.

The API should also have a health check endpoint to verify server startup.

**Endpoints:**

* `GET /api/posts`: Retrieve a list of all blog posts.
* `GET /api/posts/:id`: Retrieve a single blog post by its ID.
* `POST /api/posts`: Create a new blog post.
* `PUT /api/posts/:id`: Update an existing blog post.
* `DELETE /api/posts/:id`: Delete a blog post.
* `GET /api/health`: Verify server startup.

**Constraints:**

* The database connection should be in-memory.
* Use TypeORM to interact with the database.
* Use the `express.json()` middleware to parse JSON request bodies.
* Implement pagination for the `GET /api/posts` endpoint to limit the result to 10 items per page.
* Implement error handling for cases where the requested post ID is not found.

**API Request and Response Examples:**

* `GET /api/posts`:
  + Request: No body
  + Response: `200 OK` with a list of blog posts
* `POST /api/posts`:
  + Request: `application/json` with `title`, `content`, and `author`
  + Response: `201 Created` with the created post
* `GET /api/posts/1`:
  + Request: No body
  + Response: `200 OK` with the post
* `PUT /api/posts/1`:
  + Request: `application/json` with `title`, `content`, and `author`
  + Response: `200 OK` with the updated post
* `DELETE /api/posts/1`:
  + Request: No body
  + Response: `200 OK` with no body

**Database Schema:**

The `posts` table should have the following columns:

* `id`: The unique ID of the post
* `title`: The title of the post
* `content`: The content of the post
* `author`: The author of the post
constraints: Must use only the in-memory array or mock database provided
Use TypeORM to interact with the database

endpoints.description: Retrieve a list of all blog posts
Retrieve a single blog post by its ID
